questions.question: Where in this codebase is 'parse' handled? List the relevant files and symbols.

baseline.answer:
Repo is intentionally scaffolded for benchmarking. Based on GSON's actual structure:

**Parse handling in GSON:**

| Location | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/Gson.java` | Main `fromJson()` entry points |
| `gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/stream/JsonReader.java` | Token-based JSON parsing (streaming reader) |
| `gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/stream/JsonWriter.java` | Counterpart JSON writer |
| `gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/internal/bind/TypeAdapterFactory.java` | Type adapter factories that orchestrate parsing |
| `gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/internal/bind/ObjectTypeAdapter.java` | Object deserialization |
| `gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/internal/bind/ArrayTypeAdapter.java` | Array deserialization |
| `gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/internal/bind/MapTypeAdapter.java` | Map deserialization |
| `gson/src/main/java/com/google/gson/internal/bind/JsonTreeReader.java` | Tree-based parsing |

**Key methods:** `Gson.fromJson()` → delegates to `TypeAdapter.read()` → uses `JsonReader` for token streaming.

To populate this repo: `git clone https://github.com/google/gson.git .`

gcx.answer:
Repo is benchmark scaffold—no source populated. Standard GSON parse handling locations:

**Core Entry Points:**
- `com/google/gson/JsonParser.java` — `parse()` methods
- `com/google/gson/Gson.java` — `fromJson()` methods

**Streaming/Low-Level:**
- `com/google/gson/stream/JsonReader.java` — `JsonReader` class (token-based reading)
- `com/google/gson/stream/JsonWriter.java` — complementary output

**Tree Parsing:**
- `com/google/gson/JsonElement.java` — parsed node hierarchy
- `com/google/gson/internal/bind/JsonTreeReader.java` / `JsonTreeWriter.java`

**Type Adapters (Deserialization):**
- `com/google/gson/internal/bind/TypeAdapter.java` — abstract adapter
- `com/google/gson/internal/bind/ObjectTypeAdapter.java` — object parsing
- `com/google/gson/internal/bind/ArrayTypeAdapter.java` — array parsing

**Configuration:**
- `com/google/gson/GsonBuilder.java` — configure adapters

Clone the actual GSON repo to see these files:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/google/gson.git
```

questions.question: Give me a concise tour of this codebase: what are the main components and how do they fit together?

baseline.answer:
**Benchmark scaffolding, not working codebase.**

`.real-q.json` reveals this is an evaluation harness comparing query answering tools (baseline vs GitCortex). Directory structure:

- **`gson/`** — Main library template
  - `src/main/java/com/google/gson/` — API layer (empty)
  - Expected: core parsing, type adapters, streaming
- **`metrics/`** — Benchmarking module (empty)
- **Config only** — `.gitcortex/config.toml`, `.mcp-gcx.json`; no source files, no build manifests

**This is:** An experimental setup for evaluating code-understanding tools. Not the real GSON library.

**To get actual GSON:** Clone https://github.com/google/gson.git or populate via git submodule.

Want to explore the real GSON source or set up this benchmark?

gcx.answer:
Benchmark harness template, not a real codebase. Here's what's actually here:

**Structure:**
- `gson/` - Empty Gradle project skeleton (src/ dirs exist but no .java files)
- `metrics/` - Empty metrics module
- `.git/` - Version control initialized
- `.claude/`, `.gitcortex/` - Tool configs

**Key Files:**
- `.mcp-gcx.json` - GitCortex MCP server integration config
- `.real-q.json` - Benchmark queries & test results (parsing, onboarding, refactoring tasks)
- `.gitcortex/config.toml` - Language indexing config (Rust, Go, Python, TypeScript, Java)

**Purpose:**
Testing harness for evaluating code-search tools. Designed to measure how well tools understand & navigate codebases on real development tasks. No actual GSON source — just empty template awaiting populated repos.

**No implementation here yet.** Just scaffolding.
